title: Tech Sector Volume Spike With Muted Price Change
thesis: A sudden 30%+ spike in daily volume without a corresponding 1% price move suggests accumulation or distribution, often preceding a directional breakout. Volume spikes with flat price often indicate institutional positioning in technology stocks.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily volume and price for XLK
signal_logic: Enter long if daily volume is 30% higher than 20-day average and price change is between -0.5% and +0.5%
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long if daily volume is 30% higher than 20-day average and price change is between -0.5% and +0.5% Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if price drops more than 2% from entry
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily volume and price for XLK via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volume spikes without price moves happen regularly in XLK due to sector rotation and news cycles.

market_impact.rationale: Volume spikes with flat price often indicate institutional positioning in technology stocks.

trade_logic.entry: Enter long if daily volume is 30% higher than 20-day average and price change is between -0.5% and +0.5%
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if price drops more than 2% from entry

title: Consumer Staples Sector Weekly Weather Temperature Spike
thesis: A 10%+ weekly increase in average temperature during colder months correlates with a short-term dip in staples due to reduced heating product demand. Warmer weather reduces demand for certain consumer staples like heating fuels and warm beverages.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature averaged weekly for major US metro areas
signal_logic: Enter short XLP if weekly average temperature rises >10% compared to prior week in winter months (Nov-Feb)
entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLP if weekly average temperature rises >10% compared to prior week in winter months (Nov-Feb) Exit: Exit after 3 weeks or if XLP rallies 3%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature averaged weekly for major US metro areas via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather fluctuations happen regularly and impact staples seasonally.

schema.idea: Consumer staples sector weekly weather temperature spike

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature averaged weekly for major US metro areas

schema.behavior: A 10%+ weekly increase in average temperature during colder months correlates with a short-term dip in staples due to reduced heating product demand.

market_impact.rationale: Warmer weather reduces demand for certain consumer staples like heating fuels and warm beverages.

trade_logic.entry: Enter short XLP if weekly average temperature rises >10% compared to prior week in winter months (Nov-Feb)
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 weeks or if XLP rallies 3%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather fluctuations happen regularly and impact staples seasonally.

title: Real Estate Weekly Google Trends Surge For Mortgage Rates
thesis: A 25%+ weekly spike in mortgage rate searches often signals increased market focus on housing costs, sometimes preceding real estate sector weakness due to rate concerns. Rising mortgage rate concerns weigh on real estate valuations.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly data for 'mortgage rates' US searches
signal_logic: Enter short XLRE if 'mortgage rates' search interest rises >25% week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLRE if 'mortgage rates' search interest rises >25% week-over-week Exit: Exit after 4 weeks or if XLRE rallies 3%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly data for 'mortgage rates' US searches via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Mortgage rate search interest spikes frequently in response to Fed announcements and rate moves.

title: Materials Sector Weekly Volatility Contraction Breakout
thesis: When weekly realized volatility contracts below 50% of its 20-week average, a breakout in price often follows. Volatility compression usually precedes directional moves in materials stocks.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance weekly realized volatility for XLB
signal_logic: Enter long XLB when weekly realized volatility <50% of 20-week average and price closes higher that week
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLB when weekly realized volatility <50% of 20-week average and price closes higher that week Exit: Exit after 5 weeks or if price falls 4%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance weekly realized volatility for XLB via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volatility contractions and subsequent breakouts occur regularly in materials markets.

market_impact.rationale: Volatility compression usually precedes directional moves in materials stocks.

trade_logic.entry: Enter long XLB when weekly realized volatility <50% of 20-week average and price closes higher that week
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 5 weeks or if price falls 4%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volatility contractions and subsequent breakouts occur regularly in materials markets.

title: Communication Sector Volume-price Divergence
thesis: If volume increases 25%+ compared to 20-day average but price falls less than 0.3%, it often signals accumulation ahead of a bullish move. Volume rises without price decline suggest buying interest in communication stocks.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily volume and price for XLC
signal_logic: Enter long XLC when daily volume >125% of 20-day average and price change between -0.3% and +0.3%
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLC when daily volume >125% of 20-day average and price change between -0.3% and +0.3% Exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if price falls 3%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily volume and price for XLC via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volume-price divergence is a common short-term pattern in XLC.

trade_logic.exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if price falls 3%

title: Energy Sector Sudden Weekly Volatility Spike
thesis: A 50%+ weekly spike in realized volatility often precedes strong directional moves in energy stocks. Volatility spikes frequently lead to breakouts in energy sector prices.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance weekly realized volatility for XLE
signal_logic: Enter long XLE when weekly realized volatility increases >50% compared to prior week
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLE when weekly realized volatility increases >50% compared to prior week Exit: Exit after 3 weeks or if XLE drops 5%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance weekly realized volatility for XLE via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Energy markets regularly experience volatility bursts tied to geopolitical and supply news.

market_impact.rationale: Volatility spikes frequently lead to breakouts in energy sector prices.

trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 weeks or if XLE drops 5%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Energy markets regularly experience volatility bursts tied to geopolitical and supply news.
